REMOVED FROM ARBITRATION. CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE ON JAN-27-2016 AT 10:30 AM IN DEPARTMENT 610 FOR THE SUBMISSION OF CASE MANAGEMENT STATEMENTS AND TRIAL SETTING. DEC-29-2015 ARBITRATION OSC IS OFF CALENDAR. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.

DISCOVERY, 302, DEFENDANT OAKWOOD HOTEL LLC'S MOTION TO COMPEL INITIAL RESPONSES TO FIRST SET OF FORM INTERROGATORIES UNLIMITED AND ADDITIONAL INTERROGATORIES TO PLAINTIFF VIVIAN LEE IS GRANTED. NO OPPOSITION FILED. AS TO THE REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS AGAINST PLAINTIFF VIVIAN LEE AND HER ATTORNEY OF RECORD IN THE AMOUNT OF $1810.00 [C.C.P. 2030.290], THE COURT AWARDS SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $1,000. COURT NOTES THAT NO COURTESY COPIES WERE RECEIVED PURSUANT TO LR 2.10(T) BUT COPIES OF DOCUMENTS WERE AVAILABLE ONLINE FOR THE COURT'S REFERENCE. HOWEVER, HAD THE MOTION BEEN OPPOSED, COMPLIANCE WITH LOCAL RULE 2.10(T) WOULD HAVE BEEN REQUIRED. THE PREVAILING PARTY SHALL PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER TO THE COURT FOR SIGNATURE. JUDGE PRO TEM: NILS ROSENQUEST, CLERK: SEAN KANE, NOT REPORTED. (302/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Defendant Oakwood Hotel Llcs Motion To Compel Initial Responses To First Set Of Form Interrogatories Unlimited And Additional Interrogatories To Plaintiff Vivian Lee; Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff Vivian Lee And Her Attorney Of Record In The Amount Of $1810.00 [C.C.P. 2030.290]
Off Calendar Dec-30-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT OAKWOOD HOTEL LLC'S Motion To Compel Initial Responses To First Set Of Form Interrogatories Unlimited And Additional Interrogatories To Plaintiff Vivian Lee is granted. No opposition filed. As to the request For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff Vivian Lee And Her Attorney Of Record In The Amount Of $1810.00 [C.C.P. 2030.290], the Court awards sanctions in the amount of $1,000. Court notes that no courtesy copies were received pursuant to LR 2.10(T) but copies of documents were available online for the court's reference. However, had the motion been opposed, compliance with Local Rule 2.10(T) would have been required. The prevailing party shall prepare and submit a proposed order to the Court for signature. Judge Pro Tem: Nils Rosenquest, Clerk: Sean Kane, Not Reported. (302/JPT)
